title: Area of Composite Figures
contentObjective: I can find the area of a composite figure by adding or subtracting the areas of basic shapes.
languageObjective: I can explain my steps using the words composite figure, decompose, add, and subtract.

turnAndTalk.question: The L-shaped room splits into a 12 ft by 8 ft rectangle and a 6 ft by 5 ft rectangle. Why is it easier to find the area after you decompose the L into two rectangles?
turnAndTalk.kernel: We decompose the L-shape because we already know how to find a rectangle's area.

stems.en: We decompose the L-shape into ___ because ___.
stems.es: Descomponemos la forma de L en ___ porque ___.

stems.en: After I split it, I can find each area using ___.
stems.es: Después de dividirla, puedo hallar cada área usando ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain that the L-shape is hard to measure directly, so breaking it into two rectangles lets them use A = l x w on each piece and add the results.
turnAndTalk.extend: The wonder prompt asks if there is more than one way to split the L. Show a different decomposition and justify why both give the same total area.
extendStems: Another way to split it is ___, which gives ___ + ___.
Both ways give the same total because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: For the U-shaped pool you subtracted a 6 ft by 4 ft cutout from a 14 ft by 10 ft rectangle. How do you decide when to ADD areas and when to SUBTRACT?
turnAndTalk.kernel: We add when shapes are joined and subtract when a piece is removed.

stems.en: I subtracted the areas because a piece was ___, so 140 minus 24 equals ___.
stems.es: Resté las áreas porque una parte fue ___, entonces 140 menos 24 es ___.

stems.en: I add areas when ___, but I subtract when ___.
stems.es: Sumo áreas cuando ___, pero resto cuando ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: A strong answer states you subtract when a piece is cut out (pool = 140 - 24 = 116 sq ft) and add when shapes are joined, with a clear reason for each.
turnAndTalk.extend: Could you find the U-shaped pool's area by ADDING rectangles instead of subtracting a cutout? Justify whether both strategies reach the same answer.
extendStems: I could add by splitting the pool into ___, which gives ___.

turnAndTalk.question: The T-shaped conference room has a top of 14 ft by 4 ft and a stem of 6 ft by 10 ft, and carpet costs $5 per sq ft. Talk through finding the total cost.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Find each rectangle's area, add them, then multiply by the price.

stems.en: The top area is ___ square feet and the stem area is ___ square feet.
stems.es: El área de arriba es ___ pies cuadrados y el área del tronco es ___ pies cuadrados.

stems.en: I added the areas to get ___ square feet.
stems.es: Sumé las áreas para obtener ___ pies cuadrados.

stems.en: The cost is ___ times $5, which equals $___.
stems.es: El costo es ___ por $5, que es $___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute top = 56 sq ft, stem = 60 sq ft, total = 116 sq ft, and cost = 116 x $5 = $580, explaining the add-then-multiply order.
turnAndTalk.extend: A house-shaped wall is a rectangle topped with a triangle. Explain how composite-figure thinking handles a figure made of two DIFFERENT shape types, not just rectangles.
extendStems: I would find the rectangle area with ___ and the triangle area with ___.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on Area of Composite Figures, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Break the figure into simple shapes. ADD the areas when shapes are joined; SUBTRACT when a piece is cut out.

stems.en: I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.G.1 — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

vocabulary.definition: A shape made by putting two or more simple shapes together.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una figura formada al juntar dos o más figuras simples.
vocabulary.visual: An L-shaped room = a 12×8 rectangle joined to a 6×5 rectangle; total area = 96 + 30 = 126 sq ft
vocabulary.cloze: A shape made of two or more basic shapes combined is a ___.

examples.text: An L-shaped room made of two rectangles
examples.why: It is built from two basic shapes.
One basic shape alone is not composite.

vocabulary.definitionVi: Hình dạng được tạo thành từ hai hay nhiều hình học đơn giản.
vocabulary.definitionAr: شكل هندسي يتكون من شكلين بسيطين أو أكثر.

vocabulary.definition: To break a shape into smaller, simpler shapes.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Separar una figura en figuras más pequeñas y simples.
vocabulary.visual: Draw a dashed line across the L-shape to split it into two rectangles — now you can find each area separately
vocabulary.cloze: To break a composite figure into basic shapes is to ___ it.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Tách một hình hoặc số thành các phần nhỏ hơn.
vocabulary.definitionAr: تجزئة الشكل أو الرقم إلى أجزاء أصغر.

vocabulary.definition: Add up the areas of the smaller shapes to get the total.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Sumar las áreas de las figuras pequeñas para obtener el total.
vocabulary.visual: T-shaped hallway: top rectangle = 30 sq ft, bottom rectangle = 28 sq ft → total = 30 + 28 = 58 sq ft
vocabulary.cloze: To find the total area of separate parts, I ___ their areas.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Kết hợp hai hoặc nhiều số để tìm tổng.
vocabulary.definitionAr: جمع رقمين أو أكثر لإيجاد المجموع.

vocabulary.definition: Take away the area of a missing piece from a bigger shape.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Quitar el área de una parte que falta de una figura más grande.
vocabulary.visual: A 14×10 pool with a 6×4 cutout: 140 − 24 = 116 sq ft of water surface
vocabulary.cloze: To find the area of a shape with a piece removed, I ___ the missing part's area.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Bớt đi một đại lượng từ một lượng khác để tìm hiệu.
vocabulary.definitionAr: أخذ كمية من كمية أخرى لإيجاد الفرق.

vocabulary.definition: A math rule written with symbols.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una regla matemática escrita con símbolos.
vocabulary.visual: Rectangle: A = l × w; Triangle: A = ½ × b × h; use the right formula for each piece of a composite figure
vocabulary.cloze: A math rule written with symbols is a ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Quy tắc toán học được biểu diễn bằng các ký hiệu và biến số.
vocabulary.definitionAr: قاعدة رياضية يتم التعبير عنها باستخدام الرموز والمتغيرات.

launch.narrative: Your architecture firm is calculating the floor area of an L-shaped room for a client's new home. The contractor needs the total area to order the correct amount of flooring. The room can be split into two rectangles: one is 12 feet by 8 feet and the other is 6 feet by 5 feet.
launch.contextImage: Architectural floor plan showing an L-shaped room with dimensions labeled
noticePrompts: What does the shape of the room look like?
Can you see simpler shapes inside the L-shape?
What measurements are given for each part?
wonderPrompts: Is there more than one way to split this shape into rectangles?
Could you use subtraction instead of addition to find the area?

conceptIntro.heading: How do we find the area of a composite figure?
conceptIntro.intro: A composite figure is a shape made of two or more simple shapes joined together. We decompose it into basic shapes, find each area, then add or subtract.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: Break the figure into simple shapes. ADD the areas when shapes are joined; SUBTRACT when a piece is cut out.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: My L-shaped room splits into two rectangles: one is 12 ft by 8 ft, the other is 6 ft by 5 ft.
First rectangle: A = length × width = 12 × 8 = 96 square feet.
The pieces are joined, so I add: 96 + 30 = 126.
So the total area is 126 square feet.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Now an L-shape made of a 5 ft by 4 ft rectangle and a 3 ft by 2 ft rectangle.
Are they joined or cut out? Joined, so we add: 20 + 6 = 26 square feet.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: Next you will find areas for an L-shape, a T-shape, and a U-shaped pool with a cutout.
Remember: add joined pieces, but subtract a piece that is cut out.

explore.instructions: Break each composite figure into simpler shapes. Find the area of each part, then calculate the total area.
explore.label: Decompose each composite figure and calculate the total area.

discourse.prompt: For the U-shaped pool, why did we subtract instead of add? When do you add areas and when do you subtract?
discourse.sentenceFrame: I ___ the areas because the composite figure is made by ___ a piece from a larger shape, so the total area is ___ - ___ = ___ square feet.

optional.stem: Plan 1 — Office wing: the floor plan is a 7 ft by 5 ft rectangle with a triangular reading nook (base 7 ft, height 4 ft) joined on top. Decompose it and ADD the areas. What is the total floor area?
optional.explanation: Rectangle = 7 x 5 = 35 sq ft. Triangle = 1/2 x 7 x 4 = 14 sq ft. The pieces are joined, so add: 35 + 14 = 49 sq ft.

optional.stem: Plan 2 — L-shaped lobby: decompose the L into two rectangles, one 9 ft by 6 ft and one 7 ft by 3 ft, then ADD. What is the total floor area?
optional.explanation: First rectangle = 9 x 6 = 54 sq ft. Second rectangle = 7 x 3 = 21 sq ft. Joined L-shape, so add: 54 + 21 = 75 sq ft.

optional.instructions: Plan 3 — Survey the site. Drag each part of a floor plan into the correct group: do you ADD it to the total or SUBTRACT it from a larger shape?

items.text: Triangular entrance canopy joined to the front wall
Square skylight opening cut out of the roof
Rectangular wing attached to the main hall
Elevator shaft removed from the office floor
Triangular gable joined on top of the rectangular wall
Circular fountain basin carved out of the courtyard

optional.stem: Plan 4 — Design studio: the floor is a 16 ft by 12 ft rectangle, but a 5 ft by 4 ft storage closet is cut out of one corner. Find the usable floor area.
optional.explanation: Large rectangle = 16 x 12 = 192 sq ft. Cutout closet = 5 x 4 = 20 sq ft. A piece is removed, so subtract: 192 - 20 = 172 sq ft.

pairs.term: House-front wall: 11x6 rectangle + triangle base 11, height 4

optional.label: Plan 5 — Match each floor plan to its correct total area.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — Catch the Architect's Area Mistake

workedExample.label: Identify the shapes
workedExample.work: A clubhouse wall is a 18 ft by 11 ft rectangle with a triangular gable (base 18 ft, height 6 ft) joined on top.

workedExample.label: Add the areas for the total

optional.correctWork: The architect used the rectangle's area twice (198 + 198). Each region's area is counted only once. Total = 198 + 54 = 252 sq ft.
hints: Look at Step 4. How many regions are in this figure, and how many areas should you add?
There are only two pieces: one rectangle (198) and one triangle (54). Adding 198 twice double-counts the rectangle.

approaching.stem: An L-shaped room is made of two rectangles: 10 ft × 6 ft and 4 ft × 3 ft. What is the total area?
approaching.explanation: Area 1 = 10 × 6 = 60 sq ft. Area 2 = 4 × 3 = 12 sq ft. Total = 60 + 12 = 72 sq ft.

approaching.stem: A rectangular patio is 15 ft × 10 ft with a 5 ft × 4 ft rectangular flower bed cut out. What is the remaining area?
approaching.explanation: Patio = 15 × 10 = 150 sq ft. Cutout = 5 × 4 = 20 sq ft. Remaining = 150 − 20 = 130 sq ft.

approaching.stem: Find the area of an L-shape made of a 5×3 rectangle and a 2×4 rectangle.
When should you subtract areas instead of adding them to find the area of a composite figure?
choices: When a piece is cut out or removed from a larger shape
When one shape is larger than the other
approaching.explanation: Subtract when a piece is removed from a larger shape (like a window cut from a wall). Add when two shapes are joined together (like an L-shape).

approaching.instructions: Sort each composite figure by whether you ADD or SUBTRACT the areas to find the total.

items.text: Wall with a window cut out

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Area of Composite Figures.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: Break the figure into simple shapes. ADD the areas when shapes are joined; SUBTRACT when a piece is cut out.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: Break the figure into simple shapes. ADD the areas when shapes are joined; SUBTRACT when a piece is cut out.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: Break the figure into simple shapes. ADD the areas when shapes are joined; SUBTRACT when a piece is cut out.

onLevel.label: Match each composite figure to its total area.

onLevel.instructions: A rectangular wall is 12 ft × 10 ft = 120 sq ft. A 4 ft × 3 ft window is cut out (12 sq ft). The bar model shows the whole (wall) and the parts (painted area + window).
onLevel.label: Wall area = 120 sq ft → painted area (108) + window cutout (12)

onLevel.instructions: Calculate each composite figure's area. Sort by whether the area is more than 100 sq ft or not.

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Area of Composite Figures.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: Break the figure into simple shapes. ADD the areas when shapes are joined; SUBTRACT when a piece is cut out.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: Break the figure into simple shapes. ADD the areas when shapes are joined; SUBTRACT when a piece is cut out.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: Break the figure into simple shapes. ADD the areas when shapes are joined; SUBTRACT when a piece is cut out.

extending.title: Find the Area Error

workedExample.label: Identify the shapes
workedExample.work: Rectangle: 16 ft × 10 ft, with a 4 ft × 6 ft piece cut from the corner

extending.correctWork: Since the piece is cut out, subtract: 160 − 24 = 136 sq ft. When a piece is removed, subtract its area from the larger shape.
hints: Look at Step 4. Was the smaller rectangle added on or cut away?
The problem says the piece was 'cut from the corner,' so you should subtract, not add.

extending.prompt: A T-shaped room can be decomposed in two different ways. Show both ways and prove they give the same total area. Use a T-shape where the top is 14 ft × 4 ft and the stem is 6 ft × 10 ft.
extending.sentenceFrame: Way 1: I split it into ___ and ___. Areas: ___ + ___ = ___. Way 2: I used a ___ rectangle and subtracted ___. Areas: ___ − ___ = ___. Both give ___ sq ft.

extending.instructions: This composite figure is a house shape: a rectangle topped with a triangle. Find each area and the total.

optionalActivity.name: Composite Figure Architect Project
optionalActivity.intro: Step into the firm and design a building floor plan by decomposing it into rectangles and triangles, then adding the areas.

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in Area of Composite Figures is skipping the key idea: "Break the figure into simple shapes. ADD the areas when shapes are joined; SUBTRACT when a piece is cut out." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: An architect is ordering carpet for a T-shaped conference room. The top of the T is 14 feet by 4 feet and the stem is 6 feet by 10 feet. Carpet costs $5 per square foot.
connect.promptQuestion: How much will the carpet cost?
connect.prompt: The top area is ___ sq ft and the stem area is ___ sq ft. Total = ___ + ___ = ___ sq ft. Cost = ___ x $5 = $___.

exitTicket.stem: A composite figure is made of a 9 ft x 7 ft rectangle and a 3 ft x 4 ft rectangle joined together. What is the total area?
exitTicket.explanation: Area 1 = 9 x 7 = 63 sq ft. Area 2 = 3 x 4 = 12 sq ft. Total = 63 + 12 = 75 square feet.
